openingQuery: These ceramics arrived chipped. What now?

chat.content: These ceramics arrived chipped. What now?

I'm sorry — that shouldn't have happened. I can help with style and recommendations, but return handling for ceramics sits outside what I can answer right now. In the workshop format you're about to build Experience Guide, which will own this flow end-to-end.

plan.flowSummary: Dispatcher → support intent matched → Experience Guide stubbed → voice-matched non-answer

meta.note: Workshop format only — Experience Guide is the second build. In the builder's session format, Experience Guide ships pre-applied and this session resolves cleanly.

telemetry.title: Routing fall-through — Experience Guide stubbed
telemetry.description: Workshop format: Experience Guide is the second build after Stock Keeper. Builder's session format: this agent is pre-applied and the session resolves.

sections.title: Honest gap
paragraphs: Theo's ceramics arrived chipped and he asked the obvious question. The Dispatcher matched support intent correctly, but Experience Guide is in stub state in the workshop format — so the system returned a voice-matched non-answer rather than walk him through a return.
In the builder's session, Experience Guide ships pre-applied and this session resolves with a full return-window explanation and a prepaid label. Two format behaviors, one teaching surface.
